result.stdout:
DraftSpec - RSpec-style testing for .NET

Usage:
  draftspec run <path> [options]   Run specs once and exit
  draftspec watch <path>           Watch for changes and re-run
  draftspec init [path]            Initialize spec infrastructure
  draftspec new <name> [path]      Create a new spec file

Options:
  --format, -f <format>  Output format: console, json, markdown, html
  --output, -o <file>    Write output to file instead of stdout
  --parallel, -p         Run spec files in parallel
  --css-url <url>        Custom CSS URL for HTML output
  --force                Overwrite existing files (for init)
  --no-cache             Disable dotnet-script caching

Coverage Options:
  --coverage             Enable code coverage collection
  --coverage-output <dir>  Coverage output directory (default: ./coverage)
  --coverage-format <fmt>  Coverage format: cobertura, xml (default: cobertura)
  --coverage-report-formats <formats>  Additional report formats: html, json

Path can be:
  - A directory (runs all *.spec.csx files recursively)
  - A single .spec.csx file
  - Omitted (defaults to current directory)

Examples:
  draftspec init
  draftspec new Calculator
  draftspec run ./specs
  draftspec run ./specs --format markdown -o report.md
  draftspec run ./specs --coverage
  draftspec watch .
